blocks.text: DTC P0615: Starter relay drive circuit failure

The starter relay drive circuit consists of two starter relays connected in series (starter cut relay 1 and starter cut relay 2) to prevent the starter from continuing to rotate due to a starter relay ON failure. A diagnostic line (STRLD DIAG line) is provided to monitor the voltage between the switch contacts of these two starter relays, and the AD values are captured in the PGM-FI ECU. The PGM-FI ECU is configured to detect diagnostic line disconnections and starter relay switch contact failures. In addition, a voltage monitoring circuit for the output terminals of the starter relay (ST CUT RLY1 CL- line and ST CUT RLY2 CL- line) is provided as a condition for detecting a failure. When the drive instructions for starter cut relay 1 and starter cut relay 2 are ON and the voltage on the diagnostic line does not exceed the predetermined range for a predetermined period of time, the PGM-FI ECU determines a failure and stores a DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows: Ignition switch/ ENGINE START STOP switch

blocks.text: When the drive indication for starter cut relay 1 and starter cut relay 2 is ON and the voltage on the diagnostic line (START DIAG line) is 2.4 V or higher and 2.6 V or lower for 0.3 seconds or longer.

items.text: Disconnection of diagnostic line (START DIAG line)

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the DTC and freeze data are stored without turning on the PGM-FI warning light.
Temporary DTCs, DTCs and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
